arc_title: Who is built to sustain performance and who can swing a series?

steps.narrative: Top-5 race is primed for fan-facing weekly drama: The top of the board is tight enough to produce meaningful weekly movement and marquee matchups.
steps.transition: Start with what the audience immediately cares about.

steps.narrative: Texas Tech leads the all-around profile: Texas Tech ranks first by composite score, signaling balanced strength across offense, pitching, discipline, and defense.
steps.transition: Ground the hook in measurable context.

steps.narrative: Alabama A&M shows one of the most balanced profiles: Alabama A&M has near-matching offense and pitching z-scores, which usually translates to stable game-to-game performance.
steps.transition: Introduce standout individuals to create contrast.

steps.narrative: Megan Grant is the top OPS impact bat: Megan Grant currently sets the pace in OPS among qualified hitters, indicating elite on-base plus power production.
Megan Grant leads the long-ball race: Megan Grant leads qualified hitters in home runs, creating game-changing swing outcomes.
steps.transition: End with a practical watch-list or action cue.

steps.narrative: Maya Johnson is the top bat-missing arm: On a workload-adjusted basis, Maya Johnson leads qualified pitchers in strikeouts per 7 innings.
Texas Tech profiles as a coaching-efficiency standout: Texas Tech combines strong discipline and defensive execution, a common marker of repeatable coaching impact.
Kansas owns the strongest run-prevention signal: Kansas ranks near the top in both ERA and WHIP, suggesting dependable prevention quality.
